meaning: adj.秘密从事的,秘密的,被雇进行间谍活动的
examples: Officers had tailed the gang during a major undercover operation.
Journalists informed police who planted an undercover detective to trap Smith.
Swanson persuaded Hubley to work undercover to capture the killer.
Investigations by Britain's domestic security service, MI5, can include undercover surveillance, phone tapping and communications intercepts.
The exceptions: the Academy Awards and Undercover Boss, which aired immediately after the Super Bowl.
